member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that compiler support for a particular feature is required for the location where this attribute is applied.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initialize a new instance of

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.FeatureName
member.summary: The name of the compiler feature.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.IsOptional
member.summary: If true, the compiler can choose to allow access to the location where this attribute is applied if it does not understand .

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RefStructs
member.summary: The used for the ref structs C# feature.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RequiredMembers
member.summary: The used for the required members C# feature.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DisableRuntimeMarshallingAttribute
member.summary: Disables the built-in runtime managed/unmanaged marshalling subsystem for P/Invokes, Delegate types, and unmanaged function pointer invocations.
member.remarks:
The built-in marshalling subsystem has some behaviors that cannot be changed due to backward-compatibility requirements. This attribute allows disabling the built-in subsystem and instead uses the following rules for P/Invokes, Delegates, and unmanaged function pointer invocations:
- All value types that do not contain reference type fields recursively (unmanaged in C#) are blittable - Value types that recursively have any fields that have [StructLayout(LayoutKind.Auto)] are disallowed from interop. - All reference types are disallowed from usage in interop scenarios. - SetLastError support in P/Invokes is disabled. - varargs support is disabled. - LCIDConversionAttribute support is disabled.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute
member.summary: Indicates which arguments to a method involving an interpolated string handler should be passed to that handler.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the argument that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The names of the arguments that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerArgumentAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Gets the names of the arguments that should be passed to the handler.
member.remarks: may be used as the name of the receiver in an instance method.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InterpolatedStringHandlerAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the attributed type is to be used as an interpolated string handler.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a type has required members or that a member is required.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.OSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Base type for all platform-specific API attributes.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.ObsoletedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Marks APIs that were obsoleted in a given operating system version.
member.remarks: Primarily used by OS bindings to indicate APIs that should not be used anymore.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.SupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates a custom guard field, property or method with a supported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple supported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements in order to safely call platform specific APIs.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.UnsupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates the custom guard field, property or method with an unsupported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple unsupported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements as a guard to safely call APIs unsupported on those platforms.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.SetsRequiredMembersAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that this constructor sets all required members for the current type, and callers do not need to set any required members themselves.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the syntax used in a string.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Syntax
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the syntax used.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.CompositeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing composite formats for string formatting.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateTimeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date and time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.EnumFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.GuidFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Json
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing JavaScript Object Notation (JSON).

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.NumericFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing numeric format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Regex
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing regular expressions.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeSpanFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Uri
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing URIs.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Xml
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing XML.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.StackTraceHiddenAttribute
member.summary: Types and Methods attributed with StackTraceHidden will be omitted from the stack trace text shown in StackTrace.ToString() and Exception.StackTrace

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Nanoseconds(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Gets the nanosecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Nanosecond(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Gets the nanosecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Nanosecond(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Gets the nanosecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Microseconds(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Gets the microsecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Microsecond(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Gets the microsecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.Microsecond(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Gets the microsecond component of the time represented by the current object.

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.AddMicroseconds(System.DateTime,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new object that adds a specified number of microseconds to the value of this instance..

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.AddMicroseconds(System.DateTimeOffset,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new object that adds a specified number of microseconds to the value of this instance..

member.name: M:PolyfillExtensions.ReadToEndAsync(System.IO.StreamReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads all characters from the current position to the end of the stream asynchronously and returns them as one string.
member.param: The token to monitor for cancellation requests.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous read operation. The value of the TResult parameter contains a string with the characters from the current position to the end of the stream.
member.exception: The number of characters is larger than .
The stream reader has been disposed.
The reader is currently in use by a previous read operation.

member.name: T:DiffEngine.DiffRunner
member.summary: Manages diff tools processes.

member.name: M:DiffEngine.DiffRunner.Launch(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Launch a diff tool for the given paths.

member.name: M:DiffEngine.DiffRunner.LaunchAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Launch a diff tool for the given paths.

member.name: M:DiffEngine.DiffRunner.Kill(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Find and kill a diff tool process.

member.name: M:DiffEngine.ProcessCleanup.Kill(System.String)
member.summary: Find a process with the matching command line and kill it.

member.name: M:DiffEngine.ProcessCleanup.FindAll
member.summary: Find all processes with `% %.%.%` in the command line.

member.name: P:DiffEngine.ProcessCommand.Command
member.summary: The command line used to launch the process.

member.name: P:DiffEngine.ProcessCommand.Process
member.summary: The process Id.
